response:
source
The function begins by initializing two variables to track the sum of squares and the product of cubes, setting the sum to zero and the product to one as required for empty sets.
***
It then iterates through each integer in the provided slice, calculating the square of the current number and adding it to the running sum of squares.
***
During the same iteration loop, the code computes the cube of the current number and multiplies it with the running product of cubes to update the total.
***
If the input slice contains no elements, the loop does not execute, leaving the sum at zero and the product at one, which matches the specified base case behavior.
***
After processing all elements, the function constructs and returns an ordered pair containing the final sum of squares as the first element and the final product of cubes as the second.
***
This implementation ensures that the mathematical operations are performed correctly for any set of whole numbers while adhering to the defined edge cases and return format.
